source_jobs.title: Foward Deployed Engineer - Enterprise
source_jobs.description:
About Titan

Titan is building the AI workforce for IT. We deploy AI agents across our portfolio of Managed Service Providers and enterprise customers: agents that monitor, triage, and resolve work across the service desk, infrastructure, security, identity, endpoint, and application operations. The result is faster resolutions, better uptime, and more proactive, personalized support, delivered 24/7.

In under a year we've raised over $100M from General Catalyst, 8VC, and CalPERS, acquired market-leading MSPs like RFA, and put agents into production in large enterprises. We've automated 100K+ tickets across 400+ customers. Our tech team has talent from Scale AI, CoreWeave, Vanta, Dropbox, Meta, FiveRings, TwoSigma, Harvard, Stanford, and MIT.

Joining Titan means helping redefine how work gets done in IT services, and building a $100B company.



About the Role

As a Forward Deployed Engineer, you own client engagements end to end, operating at the intersection of IT operations and the Titan AI platform. You bring clients onto Titan: learning their environment, mapping their systems, encoding their policies, and standing up the engagement. From there you're their single point of contact through onboarding and strategic projects, driving both the relationship and the technical work.

This role is focused on serving our enterprise clients.



What You'll Do

 - Own customer engagements end to end: design and deliver implementations that align Titan to each customer's workflows and goals.

 - Build AI agents: design and build agents on Titan's platform that drive real value for customers.

 - Lead migrations: design and execute migration plans that minimize disruption and ensure seamless adoption of Titan's platform.

 - Partner cross-functionally: work with Engineering, Sales, and Marketing to turn field learnings into repeatable processes and a sharper go-to-market.



Who You Are

 - 3+ years in a deployed, field, or solutions-engineering role

 - A natural owner. You run the relationship and the timeline without being told to, and clients trust you in the room.

 - Comfortable with ambiguity and speed. You'd rather ship and iterate than wait for a perfect spec.

 - Technical depth to work hands-on: proficient with scripting and APIs, with the aptitude to learn identity, cloud, and security/endpoint tooling.

 - Fluent alongside AI tools, and able to help design AI-native workflows and tooling.

 - Bonus: hands-on experience in the infosec sector

 - Bonus: Experience dealing with Enterprise customers

source_jobs.title: Staff Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
STAFF PLATFORM ENGINEER

About Titan:

Titan is an AI holding company transforming IT services with its Augmented AI platform. We acquire and partner with the best Managed Service Providers (MSPs) and embed AI at the core of their operations, empowering technicians to work faster, smarter, and more strategically. Our augmented AI platform enables MSPs to scale like technology companies while delivering faster resolutions, better uptime, and more proactive, personalized support for their customers.


In under a year, we’ve raised over $70M from General Catalyst, acquired market-leading MSPs like RFA, and deployed agents seeing real-world impact to productivity. Our team brings together AI product expertise from Scale AI, Google, MIT, and Stanford, with private equity experience from H.I.G. Capital and seasoned MSP executives who have led some of the industry’s top providers. Joining Titan means helping redefine how work is done in IT Services and building a $100B company.



About The Role:

As a Staff Product Engineer at Titan, you are a high-agency owner of our most ambitious product initiatives. We are looking for "engineers-as-founders" who thrive when given a high-level objective—like "Automate 40% of L1 support tickets"—and the autonomy to navigate from ideation to deployment independently. You’ll sit at the intersection of AI and real-world operations, working directly with our MSP partners to build AI-native products that solve their highest-stakes pain points. This is a "0 to 1" opportunity for a builder who wants to see their code fundamentally rewrite the economics of a multi-billion dollar industry.



What You Will Do:

 - Own major product initiatives from idea through deployment and iteration. Make trade-offs. Ship. Measure results. Improve.

 - Work closely with our AI team to integrate LLMs and autonomous agents into practical, production-grade workflows. Build interfaces that technicians rely on daily.

 - Talk to users. Observe workflows. Identify inefficiencies. Translate ambiguity into product decisions that materially improve operations across acquired MSPs.

 - Move quickly without sacrificing quality. Write clean, maintainable code. Balance speed with durability.

 - Set the standard for product quality and engineering velocity as we scale.



Who You Are:

 - You’ve built something from scratch — as a founder or early engineer. You thrive when given a goal and minimal instructions.

 - You care about outcomes, not just implementation. You think in terms of user impact and business leverage.

 - You take initiative. You don’t wait for permission. You close loops.

 - You can explain trade-offs clearly — to engineers, operators, and leadership.

 - You can build modern web applications end-to-end (e.g., React, TypeScript, Python, Node). You’re comfortable integrating AI into real systems.

Bonus:

 - Experience in highly regulated or precision-driven environments (e.g., fintech, hedge funds, complex enterprise systems).

 - Experience as an early employee at a high-growth, venture-backed startup.

source_jobs.title: Service Desk Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Service Desk Engineer, Level 1

Titan is an AI-native IT services firm. Autonomous agents resolve a growing share of tickets, and the human team handles what agents can't yet. As a Level 1 Service Desk Engineer, you're the front line. You own the tickets that reach a human, resolve them fast, and flag the patterns that should become automation.

This is not a traditional Tier 1 seat. You'll work alongside AI agents, not against them. The repetitive password resets and provisioning requests are increasingly handled by the system. Your job is to handle the cases that need judgment, escalate cleanly when they don't fit, and feed every recurring issue back to the people building the agents.

What you'll do

 - Own and resolve inbound service desk tickets across endpoint, identity, access, and connectivity issues, hitting SLA and CSAT targets.

 - Triage what comes in: resolve what you can, route what you can't, and document every step so the next person (or agent) is faster.

 - Work the escalation path between AI agents and senior engineers. Catch what agents miss, hand off what's above your tier.

 - Spot recurring issues and surface them to engineering and product so they become automated resolutions, not repeat tickets.

 - Keep documentation, runbooks, and knowledge articles current as the environment and tooling change.

What we're looking for

 - 1+ years in a service desk, help desk, or desktop support role (or equivalent hands-on experience).

 - Working knowledge of Windows, macOS, Active Directory / Entra ID, and common SaaS admin (Microsoft 365, Google Workspace).

 - Comfort with a ticketing system (ServiceNow, ConnectWise, or similar) and a habit of documenting clearly.

 - Strong written communication. You'll resolve a lot over chat and email.

 - Curiosity about automation and AI tooling, and the instinct to ask "why is this still a manual ticket?"

Nice to have

 - Scripting (PowerShell, Python) or any experience automating routine tasks.

 - Exposure to identity governance, MDM/Intune, or network basics.

 - MSP or multi-client environment experience.

Why Titan
You'll learn the service desk as it's being rebuilt around AI, not bolted onto it. Strong Level 1 engineers here grow into automation, escalation engineering, and specialist tracks fast, because the routine work keeps getting handed to the agents and the interesting work keeps landing on your desk.

source_jobs.title: Agent Product Manger
source_jobs.description:
AGENT PRODUCT MANAGER

About Titan

Titan is building the AI workforce for IT. We deploy AI agents across our portfolio of Managed Service Providers and enterprise customers: agents that monitor, triage, and resolve work across the service desk, infrastructure, security, identity, endpoint, and application operations. The result is faster resolutions, better uptime, and more proactive, personalized support, delivered 24/7.

In under a year we've raised over $100M from General Catalyst, 8VC, and CalPERS, acquired market-leading MSPs like RFA, and put agents into production in large enterprises. We've automated 100K+ tickets across 400+ customers. Our tech team has talent from Scale AI, CoreWeave, Vanta, Dropbox, Meta, FiveRings, TwoSigma, Harvard, Stanford, and MIT.

Joining Titan means helping redefine how work gets done in IT services, and building a $100B company.


About the Role

As an Agent Product Manager, you get Titan's AI agents working in our enterprise clients. You learn each environment, map the systems and workflows, encode client policies, and stand agents up in production, then tune them until they're resolving real work reliably. This is a hands-on, technical role that sits between product and the field: your job is to take agents from "configured" to "trusted and resolving tickets at scale," and to turn what you learn into a better product.

You work closely with Forward Deployed Engineers, who own the overall relationship, while you drive the agent deployment itself, from initial integration through tuning, measurement, and expansion across new workflows.


What You'll Do

 - Deploy AI agents in client environments: integrate Titan with each customer's tooling, encode their policies, and get agents resolving work in production.

 - Tune for reliability: monitor agent performance, debug failures, and iterate on prompts, workflows, and guardrails until resolution quality and trust are high.

 - Map systems and workflows: learn each client's service desk, identity, cloud, security, and endpoint stack, and design how agents fit into it.

 - Expand coverage: identify new workflows and ticket types agents can take on, and roll them out with minimal disruption.

 - Turn field learnings into product: partner with Engineering to make deployment faster and more repeatable, and feed real-world findings back into the platform and roadmap.


Who You Are

 - 3+ years in a Product Management, Forward Deployed Engineer, Solutions Architect, or similar deployment-focused role.

 - Hands-on and technical: proficient with scripting and APIs, with the aptitude to learn identity, cloud, and security/endpoint tooling.

 - Fluent alongside AI tools, and able to design and tune AI-native workflows.

 - Comfortable with ambiguity and speed. You'd rather ship and iterate than wait for a perfect spec.

 - Detail-driven and reliability-minded: you care that agents work correctly in production, not just in a demo.

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
STAFF SOFTWARE ENGINEER

About Titan:

Titan is an AI holding company transforming IT services with its Augmented AI platform. We acquire and partner with the best Managed Service Providers (MSPs) and embed AI at the core of their operations, empowering technicians to work faster, smarter, and more strategically. Our augmented AI platform enables MSPs to scale like technology companies while delivering faster resolutions, better uptime, and more proactive, personalized support for their customers.


In under a year, we’ve raised over $70M from General Catalyst, acquired market-leading MSPs like RFA, and deployed agents seeing real-world impact to productivity. Our team bring together AI product expertise from Scale AI, Google, MIT, and Stanford with private equity experience from H.I.G. Capital and seasoned MSP executives who have led some of the industry’s top providers. Joining Titan means helping to redefine how work is done in IT Services and building a $100B company.



About The Role:

As a Staff Software Engineer at Titan, you’ll play a pivotal role in shaping the future of AI-powered IT service delivery. You’ll work across the stack to build intuitive, high-impact products that blend human expertise with autonomous AI agents, transforming how managed service providers (MSPs) operate. This is an opportunity to join an early-stage team, have meaningful product ownership, and help define the engineering culture as we scale.



What You Will Do:

 - Design, build, and maintain scalable backend systems that power AI-driven automation.

 - Develop APIs and services that integrate AI models into customer-facing applications.

 - Collaborate with AI and frontend engineers to integrate our AI-powered platform into customer-facing tools.

 - Drive technical decisions around system architecture, scalability, and best practices as we scale.

 - Ship fast and iterate, balancing speed with quality in a fast-paced, startup environment.



Who You Are:

 - 4+ years of software engineering experience, with expertise in building and scaling web services.

 - Strong proficiency in Python, Node.js, or Go, with experience in cloud-based architectures (AWS, GCP, or Azure).

 - Familiarity with AI/ML-driven systems or real-time data processing is a plus.

 - User-focused builder - you care deeply about the customer experience and sweat the details.

 - Startup mindset - comfortable with ambiguity, eager to take ownership, and ship quickly.

 - Bonus: Experience integrating LLMs, AI-powered automation, or observability tools is a plus, but not required.

source_jobs.title: Solutions Architect
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT TITAN

Titan is building the AI workforce for IT. We deploy AI agents across our portfolio of Managed Service Providers and enterprise customers: agents that monitor, triage, and resolve work across the service desk, infrastructure, security, identity, endpoint, and application operations. The result is faster resolutions, better uptime, and more proactive, personalized support, delivered 24/7.

In under a year we've raised over $100M from General Catalyst, 8VC, and CalPERS, acquired market-leading MSPs like RFA, and put agents into production in large enterprises. We've automated 100K+ tickets across 400+ customers. Our tech team has talent from Scale AI, CoreWeave, Vanta, Dropbox, Meta, FiveRings, TwoSigma, Harvard, Stanford, and MIT.

Joining Titan means helping redefine how work gets done in IT services, and building a $100B company.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Solutions Architect, you own the technical shape of client engagements, operating at the intersection of IT operations and the Titan AI platform. You lead the architecture from first conversation to production: learning each environment, mapping systems and integrations, designing how Titan's agents plug into a customer's stack, and encoding their policies into a solution that scales. You are the technical authority customers trust and the bridge between what the field needs and what the platform can do.

This role is focused on serving our enterprise clients.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the solution architecture end to end: translate each customer's environment, workflows, and goals into a concrete design for deploying Titan's AI agents.

 - Design and build AI agents: architect and stand up agents on Titan's platform that drive real, measurable value across the service desk, identity, cloud, security, and endpoint operations.

 - Lead technical integrations and migrations: design integration and migration plans across identity, cloud, and security/endpoint tooling that minimize disruption and ensure seamless adoption.

 - Be the technical point of contact: guide customers through architecture reviews, scoping, and strategic projects, and earn their trust in the room.

 - Partner cross-functionally: work with Engineering, Sales, and Marketing to turn field learnings into reusable reference architectures, repeatable processes, and a sharper go-to-market.


WHO YOU ARE

 - 3+ years in a solutions architecture, solutions engineering, or deployed/field engineering role.

 - A natural owner. You drive the technical design and the timeline without being told to, and customers trust your judgment.

 - Comfortable with ambiguity and speed. You'd rather ship and iterate than wait for a perfect spec.

 - Technical depth to work hands-on: proficient with scripting and APIs, with the aptitude to learn identity, cloud, and security/endpoint tooling.

 - Fluent alongside AI tools, and able to design AI-native workflows, integrations, and reference architectures.

 - Bonus: hands-on experience in the infosec sector.

 - Bonus: experience architecting solutions for enterprise customers.

source_jobs.title: Senior Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR PLATFORM ENGINEER

About Titan:

Titan is an AI holding company transforming IT services with its Augmented AI platform. We acquire and partner with the best Managed Service Providers (MSPs) and embed AI at the core of their operations, empowering technicians to work faster, smarter, and more strategically. Our augmented AI platform enables MSPs to scale like technology companies while delivering faster resolutions, better uptime, and more proactive, personalized support for their customers.


In under a year, we’ve raised over $70M from General Catalyst, acquired market-leading MSPs like RFA, and deployed agents seeing real-world impact to productivity. Our team brings together AI product expertise from Scale AI, Google, MIT, and Stanford, with private equity experience from H.I.G. Capital and seasoned MSP executives who have led some of the industry’s top providers. Joining Titan means helping redefine how work is done in IT Services and building a $100B company.



About The Role:

As a Senior Platform Engineer at Titan, you will be responsible for building and scaling the infrastructure that supports our AI-driven products. You will own cloud architecture, security standards, and internal developer tooling, ensuring our teams can deliver features quickly and safely. As one of the first platform-focused engineers, you will directly shape how we scale our systems and integrate new MSP environments into Titan’s core platform.



What You Will Do:

 - Own our cloud infrastructure end-to-end. Design and operate it through code, not dashboards. Build systems that can handle the scale, security, and data complexity of an AI-native platform.

 - Set the bar for product security. Embed security into architecture, pipelines, and agent workflows from day one. Ensure our AI systems and data environments meet the trust expectations of the MSPs we integrate.

 - Build the paved path. Opinionated tooling, hardened CI/CD, clear deployment standards.

 - Implement production-grade monitoring, logging, and alerting. Engineer for uptime. Detect issues early. Treat reliability as a feature, not an afterthought.

 - Make long-term decisions that hold up under scale. Optimize for modularity, cost discipline, and operational stability as we grow through Series A and beyond.



Who You Are:

 - 4+ years of software engineering experience, specifically having spent time on a Platform, Core, or Infrastructure team at a startup during its high-growth Series A or B phase.

 - You have either worked as a Product Security Engineer at a startup or have a deep professional obsession with building secure-by-default systems.

 - Strong proficiency in Python, Go, or Node.js. You view infrastructure through the lens of software engineering, not just "ops."

 - You’ve seen how systems break under scale and know how to proactively design against those failures.

 - You are comfortable with ambiguity and are excited by the prospect of being the first person to "own" the platform domain at Titan.

source_jobs.title: New Grad Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
NEW GRAD PRODUCT ENGINEER

About Titan:

Titan is an AI holding company transforming IT services with its Augmented AI platform. We acquire and partner with the best Managed Service Providers (MSPs) and embed AI at the core of their operations, empowering technicians to work faster, smarter, and more strategically. Our augmented AI platform enables MSPs to scale like technology companies while delivering faster resolutions, better uptime, and more proactive, personalized support for their customers.


In under a year, we’ve raised over $70M from General Catalyst, acquired market-leading MSPs like RFA, and deployed agents who are seeing real-world impact on productivity. Our team brings together AI product expertise from Scale AI, Google, MIT, and Stanford, with private equity experience from H.I.G. Capital and seasoned MSP executives who have led some of the industry’s top providers. Joining Titan means helping to redefine how work is done in IT Services and building a $100B company.



About The Role:

We are looking for a "high-slope" New Grad who doesn't just want to code, but wants to build products that people actually use. As a New Grad Product Engineer at Titan, you’ll be paired with seasoned Senior Product Engineers (many of whom are ex-founders) to take on major product initiatives. This isn't a "rotational program" or a "shadowing" role - you will be expected to ship production code, talk to users, and iterate on AI-native features from day one.



What You Will Do:

 - Contribute to production features that integrate AI directly into technician workflows. Code that ships. Code that gets used.

 - Participate in user interviews and feedback sessions. Understand the operational problems behind what we’re building.

 - Own small-to-medium features end-to-end, backend logic, frontend implementation, and iteration after launch.

 - Learn how to balance speed with quality. Experience the 0→1 phase inside a fast-scaling company.
   

Who You Are:

 - You’ve built something outside of class. A SaaS tool. A trading bot. A mobile app. A community product. Something with real users.

 - You’ve handled bug reports. Shipped updates. Felt the pressure of real feedback.

 - Pursuing or recently completed a CS degree from a rigorous program. You understand fundamentals - data structures, systems, and architecture.

 - Comfortable with modern web technologies (TypeScript, Python, React). Curious about AI systems and how LLMs change product design.

 - You don’t wait for perfect specs. You ask questions, unblock yourself, and move.

 - Bonus: You’ve built something that generated revenue.

 - Bonus: You’ve worked with AI/ML frameworks or integrated LLM APIs into real applications.

source_jobs.title: AI-Native Service Desk Manager
source_jobs.description:
AI-Native Service Desk Manager




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're building a managed service provider where AI agents handle the bulk of frontline IT work, and humans run the system that makes that possible. We're looking for a Service Desk Manager who can operate in that environment: someone who understands traditional service desk fundamentals but is excited to lead a desk where autonomous agents resolve a growing share of tickets, and the manager's job shifts from queue-watching to orchestration, escalation design, and continuous improvement.

This isn't a legacy ITSM role with an "AI initiative" bolted on. The AI is the operating model.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own day-to-day service desk operations, hitting SLA, CSAT, and resolution-time targets across a hybrid human and AI workforce

 - Manage and develop a team of service desk engineers, defining where humans add the most value as autonomous resolution rates climb

 - Design and refine escalation paths between AI agents and human engineers, deciding what agents handle end-to-end, what gets handed off, and how

 - Monitor agent performance the way you'd manage a team: accuracy, deflection, false escalations, and customer experience

 - Partner with engineering and product to identify automation gaps and feed real ticket data back into agent improvement

 - Run reporting and analytics on desk health, autonomous resolution rate, and trend lines that inform staffing and roadmap

 - Own incident communication and major-incident coordination



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years in service desk or IT support, with 2+ years managing a team

 - Strong grounding in ITSM fundamentals (ITIL concepts, ticketing, SLA management, escalation design)

 - Comfort with metrics and data. You reason about operations through dashboards, not gut feel

 - Genuine curiosity about AI/automation in IT operations; bonus if you've worked alongside AI tooling or agents

 - Clear communicator who can manage both a team and customer relationships

 - Based in or near Raleigh and able to be onsite 3 days/week



NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in healthcare or a regulated industry

source_jobs.title: Head of Growth
source_jobs.description:
Location: San Francisco / New York




ABOUT TITAN

Titan is building the next-generation IT services platform through the acquisition and transformation of high-quality Managed Service Providers. We embed AI agents directly into service workflows, systematically improving service quality, efficiency, and margins.

Rather than selling software into MSPs, Titan operates the services layer itself—creating an AI-native operating system for SMB and mid-market IT infrastructure. Our long-term ambition is to scale this model across MSPs, MSSPs, and adjacent IT services, building a $100B+ category-defining company.




ROLE OVERVIEW

We are seeking a Head of Growth to build and scale Titan’s end-to-end growth engine.

You will own how Titan acquires, activates, expands, and retains customers across priority verticals and portfolio companies. This role translates Titan’s platform and operational advantages into durable revenue growth and strong unit economics.

You will work closely with Product, Sales, Operations, and Executive Leadership.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build and own Titan’s full growth funnel (acquisition → activation → expansion → retention)

 - Design scalable acquisition and demand systems across channels

 - Improve onboarding, retention, and expansion performance

 - Establish experimentation, analytics, and growth reporting

 - Standardize growth playbooks across acquired companies

 - Accelerate post-acquisition GTM and funnel integration

 - Hire and lead a high-performing growth team
   


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 7+ years in growth, performance marketing, or revenue operations

 - Proven experience scaling B2B growth engines

 - Background in B2B SaaS, services, or platform businesses preferred

 - Strong analytical and systems-thinking skills

 - Track record of owning CAC, LTV, and retention metrics

 - High ownership mindset in fast-moving environments
   


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE (12 MONTHS)

 - Repeatable growth engine operating across priority verticals

 - Improved CAC efficiency and payback

 - Higher expansion revenue and net retention

 - Faster post-acquisition funnel integration

 - Clear experimentation and optimization cadence
   


THE END STATE

This role builds the go-to-market engine that makes Titan the default IT partner for SMB and mid-market companies and defines the category of AI-native IT services. The systems created in this role power Titan’s expansion into a $100B company and form a lasting competitive moat driving pricing power and growth.

source_jobs.title: Strategic Finance Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT TITAN

Titan is an AI holding company transforming IT services with its Augmented AI platform. We acquire and partner with the best Managed Service Providers (MSPs) and embed AI at the core of their operations, empowering technicians to work faster, smarter, and more strategically. Our augmented AI platform enables MSPs to scale like technology companies while delivering faster resolutions, better uptime, and more proactive, personalized support for their customers.


In under a year, we’ve raised over $70M from General Catalyst, acquired market-leading MSPs like RFA, and deployed agents seeing real-world impact to productivity. Our team bring together AI product expertise from Scale AI, Google, MIT, and Stanford with private equity experience from H.I.G. Capital and seasoned MSP executives who have led some of the industry’s top providers. Joining Titan means helping to redefine how work is done in IT Services and building a $100B company.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re seeking a Strategic Finance Lead to partner directly with Titan’s founders and leadership team on the company’s most important financial and strategic priorities. This role will own company-wide financial planning, forecasting, and capital allocation, while also playing a critical role in evaluating acquisition opportunities and integrating new businesses into Titan’s platform. You’ll be the connective tissue between our operating companies, corporate leadership, and investors—helping ensure we deploy resources effectively to drive growth and returns.



This is a high-visibility, high-impact role for a finance leader who thrives in fast-paced, entrepreneurial environments and is excited to help scale a business at the forefront of AI and IT services.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Lead financial planning, budgeting, and forecasting across Titan and portfolio MSPs.

 - Build and maintain robust financial models to inform decision-making, capital allocation, and growth planning.

 - Partner with leadership on setting and tracking KPIs for operational performance across the portfolio.

 - Support M&A by evaluating targets, building deal models, and integrating acquisitions into corporate financials.

 - Prepare board materials, investor updates, and strategic presentations.

 - Develop analytical frameworks for evaluating new growth initiatives, pricing strategies, and investments.

 - Work cross-functionally with operations, product, and M&A to ensure financial rigor in strategic decisions.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Experience in investment banking, private equity, corporate development, strategic finance, or a high-growth operating company.

 - Exceptional financial modeling, forecasting, and analytical skills.

 - Strong business judgment and ability to translate data into actionable insights.

 - Excellent communication skills and executive presence; able to distill complex topics for senior stakeholders.

 - Highly organized, detail-oriented, and comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

source_jobs.title: Business Operations & Strategy
source_jobs.description:
About Titan:

Titan is building the AI workforce for IT. We deploy AI agents across our portfolio of Managed Service Providers and enterprise customers: agents that monitor, triage, and resolve work across the service desk, infrastructure, security, identity, endpoint, and application operations. The result is faster resolutions, better uptime, and more proactive, personalized support, delivered 24/7.

In under a year we've raised over $100M from General Catalyst, 8VC, and CalPERS, acquired market-leading MSPs like RFA, and put agents into production in large enterprises. We've automated 100K+ tickets across 400+ customers. Our tech team has talent from Scale AI, CoreWeave, Vanta, Dropbox, Meta, FiveRings, TwoSigma, Harvard, Stanford, and MIT.

Joining Titan means helping redefine how work gets done in IT services, and building a $100B company.

About The Role:

We are seeking a Business Operations & Strategy Lead to partner closely with our founders, MSP executives, and product / finance teams on special projects, operational improvements, and integration / growth strategy.

What You’ll Do:

 - Business operations: Partner with MSP executives and leaderships to identify key bottlenecks and revamp operations to enable better growth; embed within MSP operating teams to identify pain points and product gaps for engineers and managers

 - Revenue operations: Execute on initiatives to both accelerate sales growth and improve existing customer retention

 - Integration strategy: Identify opportunities to drive successful integration across finance, operations, and product for new portfolio organizations and clients

 - Corporate strategy: Support executives with data analytics and strategic projects to set quarterly business goals at the MSP and Titan level

Who You Are

 - 3–6 years of experience across business strategy, operations, consulting, and finance. Prior operating experience in a startup, PE, or high-growth technology environment is preferred

 - Strong business sense and analytics fluency

 - Fast learning curve – you thrive in ambiguous problem spaces; you learn new workflows and concepts quickly and become an SME within days or weeks

 - Exceptionally versatile – you can context switch to work well with operators at all levels of the business across multiple projects. You thrive in both high-level strategy discussion and day-to-day execution

 - Excellent executive presence and communication skills – you build working relationships with all teams, you can synthesize complex ideas, and you are calm under pressure

source_jobs.title: Vice President - M&A
source_jobs.description:
VICE PRESIDENT - M&A

About Titan
Titan is building an AI-native platform for the $300B managed IT services market, acquiring leading managed service providers (MSPs) and deploying AI agents across service desk, security, networking, server administration, and back-office operations. Today, Titan’s agents autonomously perform over 5,000 operational actions per day across customer environments. The company has raised over $90M from investors including General Catalyst, 8VC, and CalPERS, and today employs more than 350 IT professionals across its platform companies.



Our team brings together AI product expertise from Scale AI, Google, MIT, and Stanford with private equity experience from H.I.G. Capital and seasoned MSP executives who have led some of the industry’s top providers. Joining Titan means helping to redefine how work is done in IT Services and building a $100B company.



About the Role
We’re looking for a Vice President to help drive Titan’s acquisition strategy - from sourcing and evaluating potential MSP targets to supporting due diligence, deal execution, and integration planning. You’ll work closely with our leadership team and investors to identify high-quality acquisition opportunities, model their impact, and ensure smooth transitions post-close. We aim to close 20+ deals a year.



What You’ll Do

 - Identify, source, and build relationships with target MSPs and adjacent service providers.

 - Support the full deal lifecycle - from initial outreach through closing and integration.

 - Conduct detailed financial analysis, market research, and valuation modeling.

 - Coordinate due diligence across financial, legal, operational, and technical workstreams.

 - Prepare investment memos and materials for leadership and board review.

 - Collaborate with operations and product teams to ensure seamless post-acquisition integration.

 - Maintain and refine Titan’s acquisition pipeline, tracking key deal metrics.
   
   

Who You Are

 - 3+ of experience as an associate or vp level role at a top PE firm.

 - Opinionated and self driven

 - Bonus: experience in IT services, SaaS, or roll-up strategies.



This role offers a $225K–$350K salary, along with a generous equity package.

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR PLATFORM ENGINEER

About Titan:

Titan is an AI holding company transforming IT services with its Augmented AI platform. We acquire and partner with the best Managed Service Providers (MSPs) and embed AI at the core of their operations, empowering technicians to work faster, smarter, and more strategically. Our augmented AI platform enables MSPs to scale like technology companies while delivering faster resolutions, better uptime, and more proactive, personalized support for their customers.


In under a year, we’ve raised over $70M from General Catalyst, acquired market-leading MSPs like RFA, and deployed agents seeing real-world impact to productivity. Our team brings together AI product expertise from Scale AI, Google, MIT, and Stanford, with private equity experience from H.I.G. Capital and seasoned MSP executives who have led some of the industry’s top providers. Joining Titan means helping redefine how work is done in IT Services and building a $100B company.



About The Role:

As a Senior Product Engineer at Titan, you are a high-agency owner of our most ambitious product initiatives. We are looking for "engineers-as-founders" who thrive when given a high-level objective—like "Automate 40% of L1 support tickets"—and the autonomy to navigate from ideation to deployment independently. You’ll sit at the intersection of AI and real-world operations, working directly with our MSP partners to build AI-native products that solve their highest-stakes pain points. This is a "0 to 1" opportunity for a builder who wants to see their code fundamentally rewrite the economics of a multi-billion dollar industry.



What You Will Do:

 - Own major product initiatives from idea through deployment and iteration. Make trade-offs. Ship. Measure results. Improve.

 - Work closely with our AI team to integrate LLMs and autonomous agents into practical, production-grade workflows. Build interfaces that technicians rely on daily.

 - Talk to users. Observe workflows. Identify inefficiencies. Translate ambiguity into product decisions that materially improve operations across acquired MSPs.

 - Move quickly without sacrificing quality. Write clean, maintainable code. Balance speed with durability.

 - Set the standard for product quality and engineering velocity as we scale.



Who You Are:

 - You’ve built something from scratch — as a founder or early engineer. You thrive when given a goal and minimal instructions.

 - You care about outcomes, not just implementation. You think in terms of user impact and business leverage.

 - You take initiative. You don’t wait for permission. You close loops.

 - You can explain trade-offs clearly — to engineers, operators, and leadership.

 - You can build modern web applications end-to-end (e.g., React, TypeScript, Python, Node). You’re comfortable integrating AI into real systems.
   

Bonus:

 - Experience in highly regulated or precision-driven environments (e.g., fintech, hedge funds, complex enterprise systems).

 - Experience as an early employee at a high-growth, venture-backed startup.

source_jobs.title: Sourcing Analyst
source_jobs.description:
SOURCING ANALYST

About Titan
Titan is an AI holding company transforming IT services with its Augmented AI platform. We acquire and partner with the best Managed Service Providers (MSPs) and embed AI at the core of their operations, empowering technicians to work faster, smarter, and more strategically. Our augmented AI platform enables MSPs to scale like technology companies while delivering faster resolutions, better uptime, and more proactive, personalized support for their customers.


In under a year, we’ve raised over $70M from General Catalyst, acquired market-leading MSPs like RFA, and deployed agents seeing real-world impact to productivity. Our team bring together AI product expertise from Scale AI, Google, MIT, and Stanford with private equity experience from H.I.G. Capital and seasoned MSP executives who have led some of the industry’s top providers. Joining Titan means helping to redefine how work is done in IT Services and building a $100B company.



About the Role
We’re looking for a Sourcing Analyst to fuel Titan’s acquisition engine by building and managing a high-quality pipeline of potential MSP targets. You’ll be responsible for mapping markets, identifying and qualifying acquisition opportunities, and engaging with owners to start the conversation. This role is foundational to our growth strategy and offers direct exposure to the M&A team, leadership, and investors.



What You’ll Do

 - Research and map the MSP and IT services market to identify acquisition targets.

 - Build, manage, and maintain Titan’s deal sourcing pipeline and CRM.

 - Conduct company-level analysis on size, service offerings, location, and financial potential.

 - Initiate contact with potential targets via email, phone, and networking channels.

 - Qualify opportunities based on strategic fit, market position, and deal feasibility.

 - Support M&A Associate and leadership with market intel and target lists.

 - Track outreach metrics, deal progression, and conversion rates.



Who You Are

 - 3+ years of experience in business development, sourcing, investment banking, private equity, or lead generation.

 - Strong research skills and ability to synthesize market and company data into actionable insights.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills; comfortable initiating cold outreach.

 - Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple sourcing campaigns simultaneously.

 - Self-starter with a proactive mindset and comfort working in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

 - Bonus: experience in IT services, SaaS, or acquisition-driven growth environments.

source_jobs.title: Head of Marketing
source_jobs.description:
Location: San Francisco / New York




ABOUT TITAN

Titan is building the next-generation IT services platform through the acquisition and transformation of high-quality Managed Service Providers. We embed AI agents directly into service workflows, systematically improving service quality, efficiency, and margins.

Rather than selling software into MSPs, Titan operates the services layer itself—creating an AI-native operating system for SMB and mid-market IT infrastructure. Our long-term ambition is to scale this model across MSPs, MSSPs, and adjacent IT services, building a $100B+ category-defining company.




ROLE OVERVIEW

We are seeking a Head of Product Marketing to design and scale Titan’s verticalized go-to-market engines.

This role is responsible for building repeatable, industry-specific GTM systems that translate Titan’s operational and AI advantages into sustained demand, pricing power, and market leadership.

You will own how Titan wins in priority verticals (starting with financial services and SMB), how newly acquired companies are integrated into those GTM systems, and how Titan’s platform capabilities are converted into customer-relevant outcomes.

This is a strategic, high-ownership role at the center of Titan’s growth model, working directly with Product, Engineering, Sales, Operations, and Executive Leadership.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

1. Build Vertical GTM Engines

 - Design end-to-end go-to-market systems for priority segments

 - Define ICPs, personas, narratives, and sales plays

 - Create repeatable playbooks to support rapid expansion into new verticals

2. Own Positioning & Storytelling

 - Articulate Titan’s differentiated platform and services value

 - Translate AI and operational advantages into customer outcomes

 - Ensure consistent, high-quality messaging across the portfolio

3. Enable Revenue & Growth

 - Build world-class sales and GTM materials

 - Partner with Sales to improve win rates, velocity, and deal size

 - Support demand generation and account-based strategies



4. Lead Launches & Adoption

 - Drive go-to-market for new products, services, and workflows

 - Define packaging, pricing narratives, and rollout strategies

 - Accelerate internal and external adoption of new capabilities
   

5. Lead Market Insight & Cross-Functional Execution

 - Develop deep customer and competitive intelligence

 - Influence product roadmap and investment priorities

 - Partner closely with Product, Engineering, and Operations
   


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 7+ years of experience in product marketing or other marketing leadership roles

 - Experience building or scaling vertical-specific go-to-market programs

 - Strong executive-level communication skills

 - High ownership mindset with comfort in fast-moving environments




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE IN THE FIRST 12 MONTHS

 - At least two fully operational vertical GTM engines in market

 - Standardized sales and marketing playbooks adopted across portfolio

 - Faster post-acquisition GTM integration cycles

 - Improved win rates and reduced discounting

 - Clear Titan platform narrative embedded in deals

 - Marketing measurably contributing to margin expansion




THE END STATE

This role builds the go-to-market engine that makes Titan the default IT partner for SMB and mid-market companies and defines the category of AI-native IT services. The systems created in this role power Titan’s expansion into a $100B company and form a lasting competitive moat driving pricing power and growth.

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer - Financial Services
source_jobs.description:
About Titan

Titan is building the AI workforce for IT. We deploy AI agents across our portfolio of Managed Service Providers and enterprise customers: agents that monitor, triage, and resolve work across the service desk, infrastructure, security, identity, endpoint, and application operations. The result is faster resolutions, better uptime, and more proactive, personalized support, delivered 24/7.

In under a year we've raised over $100M from General Catalyst, 8VC, and CalPERS, acquired market-leading MSPs like RFA, and put agents into production in large enterprises. We've automated 100K+ tickets across 400+ customers. Our tech team has talent from Scale AI, CoreWeave, Vanta, Dropbox, Meta, FiveRings, TwoSigma, Harvard, Stanford, and MIT.

Joining Titan means helping redefine how work gets done in IT services, and building a $100B company.



About the Role

As a Forward Deployed Engineer, you own client engagements end to end, operating at the intersection of IT operations and the Titan AI platform. You bring clients onto Titan: learning their environment, mapping their systems, encoding their policies, and standing up the engagement. From there you're their single point of contact through onboarding and strategic projects, driving both the relationship and the technical work.

This role is focused on serving our financial-services clients.



What You'll Do

 - Own customer engagements end to end: design and deliver implementations that align Titan to each customer's workflows and goals.

 - Build AI agents: design and build agents on Titan's platform that drive real value for customers.

 - Lead migrations: design and execute migration plans that minimize disruption and ensure seamless adoption of Titan's platform.

 - Partner cross-functionally: work with Engineering, Sales, and Marketing to turn field learnings into repeatable processes and a sharper go-to-market.



Who You Are

 - 3+ years in a deployed, field, or solutions-engineering role

 - A natural owner. You run the relationship and the timeline without being told to, and clients trust you in the room.

 - Comfortable with ambiguity and speed. You'd rather ship and iterate than wait for a perfect spec.

 - Technical depth to work hands-on: proficient with scripting and APIs, with the aptitude to learn identity, cloud, and security/endpoint tooling.

 - Fluent alongside AI tools, and able to help design AI-native workflows and tooling.

 - Bonus: hands-on experience in the infosec sector

 - Bonus: financial-services or regulated-industry experience, or exposure to compliance posture

notes: High volume of engineering and product roles, with a focus on AI and automation. Rapid hiring pace and high startup likelihood.
